content:
# Lost Circulation

**Summary**: The loss of drilling or cementing fluids into permeable formations.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2017-ahmed|Ahmed, 2017]] addressed lost circulation issues using fiber-based additives.

- [[2019-ahmed|Ahmed, 2019]] analyzed lost circulation as a major cost and safety risk in deep, high-pressure, high-temperature (HPHT) wells.

- [[2021-alsaihati|Alsaihati, 2021]] analyzed lost circulation as a major risk to well integrity and cost.

- [[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022]] analyzed the impact of lost circulation on well integrity and cost.

## Scientific Context

- [[2017-ahmed|Ahmed, 2017]] A common challenge in drilling and cementing that can lead to well control issues.

- [[2019-ahmed|Ahmed, 2019]] analyzed lost circulation as a major cost and safety risk in deep, high-pressure, high-temperature (HPHT) wells.

- [[2021-alsaihati|Alsaihati, 2021]] analyzed lost circulation as a major risk to well integrity and cost.

- [[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022]] analyzed the impact of lost circulation on well integrity and cost.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- fluid-loss-test

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Fiber systems were shown to plug fractures and reduce non-productive time (NPT). ([[2017-ahmed|Ahmed, 2017, p. 53]])


- Lost circulation accounts for 25-40% of total drilling costs. ([[2019-ahmed|Ahmed, 2019, p. 19]])

- Classified into seepage, partial, severe, and complete based on flow rates (e.g., 50-150 bbl/hr for severe). ([[2021-alsaihati|Alsaihati, 2021, p. 20]])

- Lost circulation can cost the industry approximately $2 to $4 billion annually. ([[2022-adjei|Adjei, 2022, p. 37]])
